title: Weekly Dose of Optimism #198
gist: A roundup of frontier hardware and reindustrialization milestones: Midjourney entered medical imaging with an ultrasonic CT 'Scanner', CZ Biohub/Berkeley unveiled a laser phase plate for protein imaging, Valar Atomics' reactor went critical, and Anduril won an Air Force fighter production contract, all framed as evidence American hard-tech execution is accelerating.

insights.claim: Midjourney launched Midjourney Medical and announced the Midjourney Scanner, a whole-body ultrasound device using ultrasound plus compute to approximate MRI capability in roughly 60 seconds.
insights.speaker: Packy McCormick
insights.so_what: A profitable AI image company moving into diagnostic imaging signals AI-native entrants challenging incumbent medical-device markets.
insights.quote: something as powerful as MRI, and as casual as a trip to the spa
insights.locator: When I wrote a Deep Dive on Ezra

insights.claim: The Midjourney Scanner relies on Butterfly Network's ultrasound-on-chip technology to enable rapid whole-body scanning.
insights.speaker: Packy McCormick
insights.so_what: Butterfly Network is a key enabling supplier whose chip tech underpins new AI-imaging entrants.
insights.quote: they’re partnering with Butterfly Network, which is a fascinating company that makes ultrasound-on-chip
insights.locator: The reason they’re able to do this so quickly

insights.claim: Midjourney has not raised venture capital and is profitable, giving it the financial freedom to expand into unrelated product categories like medical imaging.
insights.speaker: Packy McCormick
insights.so_what: Bootstrapped profitability lets companies pursue high-risk diversification without investor pressure, a model worth watching.
insights.quote: Midjourney hasn’t raised VC and is profitable, which gives them the leeway to do this (or whatever they want).
insights.locator: It’s very cool to see a company branch out

insights.claim: Midjourney founder David Holz said the Scanner is the first of eight major announcements the company plans to make in 2026.
insights.speaker: Packy McCormick
insights.so_what: A pipeline of seven more announcements suggests aggressive diversification ahead, a signal for competitors and investors in adjacent markets.
insights.quote: Holz said the Scanner is the first of eight major announcements it plans to make in 2026.
insights.locator: If you look at David Holz’s bio

insights.claim: CZ Biohub and UC Berkeley, led by physicist Holger Muller, announced a Laser Phase Plate that solves the contrast problem for imaging proteins inside cells with electron microscopes.
insights.speaker: Packy McCormick
insights.so_what: A breakthrough in seeing proteins in their natural cellular environment could accelerate drug discovery and structural biology.
insights.quote: most of our cell’s proteins operate in the shadows
insights.locator: Bigggg week for seeing litttttle things

insights.claim: The laser phase plate uses a Fabry-Perot cavity that bounces a laser beam roughly 10,000 times to reach roughly 100 million times the intensity of the Sun's surface, the brightest continuous-wave laser in the world.
insights.speaker: Packy McCormick
insights.so_what: The engineering feat unlocks cryo-electron tomography and richer views of protein interactions, expanding the toolset for biotech research.
insights.quote: roughly 100 million times the intensity of the surface of the Sun — the brightest continuous-wave laser in the world
insights.locator: Müller’s solution was a so-called Fabry-Perot cavity

insights.claim: Valar Atomics' Ward 250 reactor in Emery County, Utah went critical, the second new advanced reactor to do so before America's 250th birthday on July 4, 2026.
insights.speaker: Packy McCormick
insights.so_what: Advanced fission startups are hitting real technical milestones quickly, a sign the nuclear buildout is moving from hype to deployment.
insights.quote: Valar Atomics’ Ward 250 reactor went critical

insights.claim: Valar founder and CEO Isaiah Taylor, who lacks a college degree or nuclear physics PhD, aims to print tens of thousands of reactors globally to make energy 10x cheaper than today via mass-scale nuclear fission.
insights.speaker: Packy McCormick
insights.so_what: A first-principles mass-manufacturing approach to reactors could upend nuclear cost assumptions if it scales.
insights.quote: print tens of thousands of reactors across the globe to make energy 10x cheaper than it is today with mass-scale nuclear fission
insights.locator: Now, he and the Valar team

insights.claim: The U.S. Air Force selected Anduril's FQ-44 'Fury' as one of two jets going into production for the Collaborative Combat Aircraft fleet of uncrewed autonomous wingmen.
insights.speaker: Packy McCormick
insights.so_what: A defense startup winning a production fighter contract signals real procurement displacement of legacy primes.
insights.quote: the Air Force picked Anduril’s FQ-44 “Fury” as one of two jets going into production for the CCA fleet
insights.locator: Two years is up ladies and gentlemen

insights.claim: Anduril moved from prototype award in 2024 to a production contract in June 2026, the fastest any fighter has gone from prototype to production in more than half a century, and the first time a new company has won a fighter aircraft program since the 1970s.
insights.speaker: Packy McCormick
insights.so_what: The Air Force passing over Boeing, Lockheed, and Northrop marks a structural shift in defense procurement favoring new entrants.
insights.quote: the fastest any fighter has gone from prototype to production in more than half a century
insights.locator: Anduril went from prototype award in 2024

insights.claim: The Reindustrialize conference shifted from arguing in 2024 that America should build things again to showcasing in 2026 companies actually building, with a sharper focus on execution details.
insights.speaker: Packy McCormick
insights.so_what: The reindustrialization narrative is maturing from advocacy to execution, indicating real capital and talent flowing into hard tech.
insights.quote: Reindustrialize 3.0 was packed with examples of companies actually building in America
insights.locator: This week, I went on my annual pilgrimage

insights.claim: Standard Nuclear, founded by Decisive Point's Tommy Hendrix after Reindustrialize 1.0, filed its S-1 to go public, and Not Boring Capital is an investor.
insights.speaker: Packy McCormick
insights.so_what: A nuclear startup filing to go public signals public-market appetite for advanced energy and a maturing reindustrialization investment cycle.
insights.quote: Standard Nuclear, filed its S-1 to go public
insights.locator: It’s wild how much can change in two years
